reasoning: Confirmed. ProxyUtils::get_client_ip() (class-proxyutils.php lines 37-52) iterates HTTP_CLIENT_IP and HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR before REMOTE_ADDR with no IP-format validation — sanitize_text_field() only strips HTML/whitespace. In proxy_requests() (class-proxy-mappings.php line 174) the returned value is concatenated verbatim into the X-Forwarded-For header sent to HubSpot CDN. The amplifier is the co-sent 'X-HubSpot-Trust-Forwarded-For: true' header, signalling HubSpot to honour the forged IP. Unauthenticated reachability is confirmed: proxy_requests() fires on template_redirect with no auth check, and the predefined path patterns (/_hcms/, /hs/, /hubfs/, /hs-fs/, /cs/c/, /e3t/) are matched before any mapping lookup, so any visitor can hit this code path without needing a configured proxy mapping. Attack: send GET /_hcms/any with Client-IP: 1.2.3.4 header; HubSpot CDN receives X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4, <real_ip> and may apply rate-limiting or allow-listing against 1.2.3.4. No sanitizer or guard was missed.
